evidences.title: [PDF] monad / mon token – coinbase token sales disclosure1
evidences.content: Sale: up to 7.5B MON (7.5% of initial total supply). Sale Price: $0.025 per MON. Implied Fully Diluted Value (FDV) of the Monad Network: $2.5 billion. 2 Determination of the Sale Price: Prior to the public sale on Coinbase token sales, there has been no public market for MON tokens. The price of $0.025 per MON token in the public sale is a fixed price based on a $2.5 billion fully diluted network valuation and an initial total supply of 100 billion MON tokens. The sale price was determined through a structured approach that considers several key factors, including market conditions, competitive positioning, and strategic objectives. This methodology ensures that the sale price reflects both the intrinsic value of the Monad network and broader market dynamics. The pricing strategy aims to [...] herein. At the start of Monad Public Mainnet, 50.6B tokens (50.6%) will be locked. Locked tokens cannot be staked. The intent of this is twofold: to ensure that staking rewards initially contribute to the public circulation of MON and to avoid an initial concentration of rewards among those individuals (team members and investors) involved directly with the project. All locked tokens in th...

evidences.title: Monad to raise $188m in Coinbase's first public token sale - DL News
evidences.content: On Monday, the upcoming blockchain project announced it will sell $188 million worth of tokens to retail investors on Coinbase through a newly announced token sale platform. According to Monad, the sale will help decentralise the project by distributing its MON token more broadly among its community. For retail investors, it’s another opportunity to get exposure to a high-risk bet in an unlaunched crypto project. Monad has previously raised a total of $248 million over multiple funding rounds, according to Tracxn, a market data platform. This includes a $19 million seed round in early 2023, and a $225 million Series A round that closed in March 2024. [...] Last month, rival blockchain project MegaETH sold $50 million worth of its native token at a $1 billion valuation through Sonar, a token sale venue tied to early-stage investing platform Echo. The sale’s popularity vastly exceeded expectations, attracting over $300 million worth of pledged capital. Monad’s token sale ups the ante by valuing the project at $2.5 billion. Some 7.5% of the MON token’s 100 billion starting supply is up for grabs at $0.025 per token. The MON token is used to pay gas fees on Monad. Once the blockchai...

evidences.title: Monad Token Debuts on Coinbase at $3.9 Billion Fully Diluted ...
evidences.content: Monad’s native token MON successfully launched on Coinbase with a $3.9 billion fully diluted valuation (FDV), marking a significant milestone in the cryptocurrency market. The Layer-1 blockchain project completed its initial token offering on Coinbase’s new token sale platform before transitioning to public trading. The token sale exceeded expectations by raising $269 million from more than 85,800 participants across 70+ countries. This impressive participation level demonstrates strong investor confidence in Monad’s vision and technology, despite initial concerns about the pace of the sale. evidences.title: Monad Raises 269 Million From Token Sale, Mainnet Launch Set
evidences.content: #### #### Funding and Token Sale: What’s Going On Monad has completed its token sale on Coinbase, raising $269 million from over 85,000 participants. The Ethereum-compatible layer-1 blockchain exceeded its initial fundraising goal of $187 million. The public token sale itself offers 7.5 billion MON (7.5 % of total supply) at $0.025 per token, implying a fully diluted valuation of roughly $2.5 billion if fully subscribed. The sale is being hosted on Coinbase’s newly launched regulated token-launch platform, accessible in more than 80 countries including the U.S. Participation rules include a minimum bid of $100, a maximum bid of $100,000, and a special allocation algorithm that prioritizes smaller bids to encourage broad retail access. [...] Total supply: 100 billion MON. Public sale: 7.5 billion MON (7.5%). Community airdrop: 3.3%. Ecosystem development: 38.5%. Team: 27% (vested over multiple years). Investors: 19.7%. Treasury/operational reserves: ~4%. Most tokens are locked and scheduled for long-term vesting. Staking eligibility excludes locked tokens, emphasizing alignment with network growth rather than immediate rewards. From launch, the network plans to focus on developer...

evidences.title: Monad public sale total commitments: More than $1B, bet - KuCoin
evidences.content: Would I have joined the sale myself if I could? Yeah, because I still think it has upside potential compared to $2.5b. Unfortunately, I am in Europe and not qualified. [...] Monad public sale total commitments: More than $1B, bet: NO at 58c. Easiest would be to compare it with MegaETH which raised $1,4b at a $1b val, and we have to remember that the valuation is higher, and that the sentiment was better at that time of MegaETH + that the whole Europe is banned from this sale. So far $87m has been raised, and the goal is $187.5m. It will definitely be oversubscribed, but the question is just by how much. The ICO price is closer to the pre-market price, and I think people will be more skeptical about apeing hard. On the bull side for Monad, though they have Coinbase in their back + the sale lasts for 5 more days. Absolutely not a sure bet at all, but IMO it could be worth a try. evidences.title: Monad MON Token Sale Concludes Successfully on Coinbase
evidences.content: ### Key Takeaways Coinbase executes an internal wallet migration as part of a scheduled cybersecurity routine. The migration is standard practice and unrelated to breaches, threats, or market conditions. Users are cautioned about scammers impersonating Coinbase during the wallet movement. ### Inside the Monad MON Token Sale Concludes Update The Monad MON Token Sale Concludes after a 5.5-day event hosted exclusively on Coinbase, marking a rare regulated public sale on a major U.S. exchange. The sale allocated 7.5% of the total 100 billion MON supply at $0.025 per token, placing the project at a $2.5 billion fully diluted valuation. [...] Participants committed $269 million in USDC Launches on The Sui Network"), oversubscribing the sale by 1.43x. Will most participants receive their full MON allocation? Yes. Coinbase confirmed 99% of buyers will receive the full amount requested. What is MON used for within the Monad ecosystem? MON supports governance, staking, network security, and ecosystem development. What comes after the Monad MON Token Sale concludes? Monad is expected to accelerate its mainnet launch, developer onboarding, and ecosystem growth initiatives. ## Trusted [...]...

evidences.title: Monad Token Presale Surpasses $128M Ahead of Mainnet Launch
evidences.content: The sale is the first direct token offering hosted on Coinbase. It is open to participants in over 80 countries, including the U.S. Early-stage activity was steady but slower than expected. Analysts attribute this to investors waiting for final moments to commit larger funds. Currently, the sale sees an additional $693,500 added each hour. The public sale will close on 23 November at 10:00, just ahead of Monad’s mainnet launch on 24 November 2025. ## What Monad Offers Monad is a high-performance Layer 1 blockchain. It is fully compatible with the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM). It is designed to reach 10,000 transactions per second with near-instant finality. The platform focuses on three core pillars: [...] A temporary halt occurred in March 2025 when a supermajority was reached at an epoch boundary. The issue was resolved, and stability returned. In September 2025, Monad open-sourced its execution client under the GPL-3.0 license. Previously, in September 2024, the Discord server link was temporarily compromised but later secured. ## Why the Raise Matters The presale demonstrates robust market interest in high-performance Layer 1 solutions. It reflects confidence in EVM compat...

evidences.title: Monad token climbs 46% after early dip as Coinbase-sale buyers ...
evidences.content: Advertisement Monad’s MON token traded around $0.0365 by mid-afternoon Monday, up roughly 46% from its $0.025 sale price after recovering from an early slide to about $0.02 shortly after tokens were distributed. It follows the close of Monad's week-long public token sale on Coinbase, the first conducted through the exchange’s new token-launch platform. The offering attracted $269 million in commitments from nearly 86,000 participants, a 1.43× oversubscription of the $187.5 million allocated amount. Monad Token (MON) Price Chart. Source: Coinbase Participants came from more than 70 countries, and Coinbase said internal polling showed most joined for long-term exposure rather than short-term speculation. The number of tokens you receive depends on the popularity of the sale. We use a "filling up from the bottom" allocation algorithm to ensure broad participation. Any unused funds will be returned to you. ### Supporters get rewarded. We value users who are in it for the long game. "Flipping" a token (selling or converting soon after buying it) could lead to smaller allocations in future sales. ## Launching a token? Start here. evidences.title: The ideal way to launch – introducing token sales on ...
evidences.content: We will be hosting approximately one token sale per month, to ensure each sale has the full support and attention of our platform and community, while maintaining the highest standards. In the coming months, we plan to add features to our token sales platform including limit orders and higher allocations for issuers' target user bases. This is just the beginning. With Coinbase, we’re redefining how tokens reach real users — building a fairer, more sustainable future for crypto from the ground up. Assets that launch on Coinbase token sales will be added to the Coinbase listings roadmap. To read more about our first token sale, click here. Disclosures [...] No fees for listing or participating: There are no fees to users for participating in token sales. Coinbase charges the issuer a fee as a percentage of the amount of USDC received by the issuer from the sale. The fee is for the token sale only – Coinbase does not charge listing fees. [...] CB_tokensales_blog_post_1920x1080px.png Coinbase is setting a new standard for how projects go to market, by creating a balanced and transparent token sales platform for both issuers and users. evidences.content: ## Success at Last The final number of $269 million indicates the token sale was oversubscribed, since the initial goal was to raise $187 million. Just a few days before the event concluded, though, the figures were different and quite worrying. ### You may also like: Investigators Flag Coinbase Page Asking For Seed Phrases, Tool Removed Coinbase and Bybit in Talks for Strategic Investment Partnership: Report Brian Armstrong Denies Lobbying Against Bitcoin De Minimis Tax Exemption [...] Interestingly, just a few days before the event concluded, the demand was evidently lacking, which raised some concerns within the community. ## $270M Raised From 85K participants The team behind the project announced earlier today that 85,820 participants took part in the token sale, and the total raised amount was $269 million. The co-founder, Keone Hon, noted on X that the most “important statistic” was not the millions of raised funds but the number of participants. However, Hon acknowledged that a sizeable portion is “crypto insiders” but believes that many are also newbies. He praised the team for their efforts and added that the mainnet launch is scheduled for Monday. [...] Skip to content...

evidences.title: Monad addresses Coinbase token sale slowdown as risk of ...
evidences.content: Compared to another recent token sale, Monad's public offering is underperforming. Last month, MegaETH's public sale was so oversubscribed that despite aiming to raise about $50 million, investors committed over $1.39 billion. Market watchers online have speculated that Coinbase's MON token sale being off limits to European traders may be playing a role in the lackluster sales numbers through Tuesday. Others suggested Monad's tokenomics and past raises may have turned off potential investors. [...] "The purpose of the MON token sale is to achieve the broadest distribution," Hon wrote in a post to X. "We chose Coinbase (and their allocation algorithm, which is democratic and transparent) because of their unique ability to reach an audience that we think is important to engage and re-activate. The world is a big place and it's so important to break out of the bubble." On Monday, the much-anticipated public offering of Monad’s native token via Coinbase generated about $43 million within the first half hour. Monad seeks to raise about $187 million worth of USDC, with the maximum and minimum bid set at 100,000 USDC and 100 USDC, respectively. [...] "In the MON token sale on Coinbase,...

evidences.title: Monad Co-Founder Defends Token Sale After Slow Uptake
evidences.content: The sale initially raised $43 million in just 30 minutes, but by the end of the second day had raised just $120 million or 68% of its target. This story is an excerpt from the Unchained Daily newsletter. Subscribe here to get these updates in your email for free Monad’s tokenomics document also calls for an adaptive distribution strategy to mitigate the risk of undersubscription. The project plans to redirect unsold tokens to the Ecosystem Development category, focusing on developer grants, partnerships, and marketing initiatives. “In the MON token sale on Coinbase, users get 5 1/2 days to decide whether to commit, and once they commit, they’re locked in,” explained Hon.

evidences.title: Monad Token Presale Surpasses $128M Ahead of Mainnet Launch
evidences.content: ## Community and Incentive Programmes Monad has actively engaged its community. In October 2025, the Airdrop Claim Portal distributed MON tokens to 5,500 core members and 225,000 broader participants. The Monad Momentum Program, launched in September 2025, incentivized development across the ecosystem. Initiatives like Monad Founder Residency and accelerator programs connected developers with funding and mentorship. Physical hubs, including The Studio and The Founder Lounge, provided spaces for collaboration and knowledge exchange. The community continues to support developer adoption globally. ## Testnet Performance and Historical Milestones The Monad testnet achieved strong throughput, handling over 15 million daily transactions. Active user numbers showed consistent growth. evidences.title: Monad Price Prediction: Will MON Coin Reach $1 Soon?
evidences.content: Before exchange trading began, a public sale ran between November 17 and 22, 2025 on Coinbase. Tokens sold at $0.025 each. Around 85,820 participants contributed $269 million. Demand exceeded available supply by 144%. This oversubscription signaled intense early interest and strong speculative appetite. On November 24, 2025, the mainnet launch triggered the token generation event. MON debuted on Coinbase, Bybit, Upbit, and Bithumb simultaneously. The opening price reached $0.0329, which stood above the public sale level. Minutes later, aggressive selling pushed the price below $0.025. By the end of the day, MON stabilized near $0.0251. First-day trading volume reached roughly $37.6 million. Early optimism mixed with caution as traders tested liquidity. [...] Blockchainreporter outlines a constructive base case that places MON between $0.12 and $0.18 in 2026. Their thesis assumes steady developer migration, expanding DeFi activity, and rising transaction volume. In their framework, a 5–6× increase from the $0.025 public sale price represents a realistic outcome for a Layer 1 network that achieves traction during its first operational year.
